title: Laser cordifolium
intro:
Unfortunately, I could not find any information on the plant Laser cordifolium. However, I found that Laser is a genus of flowering plants in the family Lamiaceae, commonly known as the deadnettle or woolly betony. There is a similar species called  Laggera cordifolia, commonly known as country borage. 

Here is a paragraph about Laggera cordifolium:

Laggera cordifolia, also known as country borage, is a perennial herb that belongs to the family Lamiaceae. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties, which can help alleviate various conditions such as fever, headache, and respiratory problems. The bioactive constituents of Laggera cordifolia include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and triterpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with Laggera cordifolia include infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which can be used to treat various health conditions.
